projects.name: Ghost In A Shell
projects.description: Mac OS first, Ghostty-native workspace orchestrator

updates.headline: Hardening the core experience
updates.narrative: Ghost In A Shell had a tightening-the-screws fortnight. The commits stayed close to the core experience, with reliability work and clearer documentation moving in parallel so the tool feels more operational than fragile.
updates.valueUnlocked: The product becomes safer to rely on for actual work, which is usually the threshold between “interesting” and “worth keeping around.”
focusBullets: Clarified the README and setup guidance
Stabilized core behaviour so the product is more reliable

projects.name: Deslopify
projects.description: Make the AI writing sound more humane

updates.headline: From script to shippable tool
updates.narrative: Deslopify had a packaging-and-operations fortnight. The work pushed make the AI writing sound more humane closer to something people can reliably install, run, and keep in their workflow without extra hand-holding.
updates.valueUnlocked: It is easier to drop the tool into a real workflow instead of treating it like a one-off demo or side script.
focusBullets: Made the command-line workflow more usable
Packaged a clearer public release

projects.name: Thinking Cap
projects.description: terminal sidecar for avoiding cognitive debt while you delegate all the code to AI agents

updates.headline: Pushing the product forward
updates.narrative: Thinking Cap moved forward with a practical fortnight of product work. The changes were not ornamental; they were aimed at making terminal sidecar for avoiding cognitive debt while you delegate all the code to AI agents more usable, more legible, and easier to trust.
updates.valueUnlocked: The interaction cost drops, which matters because semi-frequent tasks live or die on friction more than on feature count.
focusBullets: Smoothed the day-to-day user experience
